features.title: Alt-Svc contract map
features.description: Map Alt-Svc behavior into contract metadata and ASGI/3 compatibility behavior.

features.title: ASGI/3 extension bridge
features.description: Expose contract-native metadata and capabilities to ASGI/3 applications through documented extensions.

features.title: ASGI pathsend contract
features.description: Feature target ASGI pathsend contract imported from claims_registry.json.

features.title: ASGI2 compatibility exclusion
features.description: Tigrcorn does not support ASGI2 as a product interface; ASGI/3 is the only supported ASGI compatibility layer.

features.title: ASGI/3 app compatibility suite
features.description: Verify ASGI/3 framework applications continue to run through the compatibility layer.

features.title: ASGI/3 compatibility layer
features.description: Run ASGI/3 applications through an explicit first-class compatibility layer.

features.title: ASGI/3 hot-path isolation
features.description: Keep ASGI/3 adapter code out of the native contract hot path unless ASGI/3 compatibility is selected.

features.title: Base default audit
features.description: Feature target Base default audit imported from claims_registry.json.

features.title: Binding legality validation
features.description: Validate binding, exchange, family, and subevent combinations against tigr-asgi-contract.

features.title: Compatibility dispatch selection
features.description: Select native contract or ASGI/3 compatibility dispatch before application traffic enters the hot path.

features.title: Compatibility feature parity matrix
features.description: Maintain native contract versus ASGI/3 compatibility feature parity status.

features.title: CONNECT policy
features.description: Feature target CONNECT policy imported from claims_registry.json.

features.title: Content coding contract map
features.description: Map HTTP content-coding behavior into contract metadata and ASGI/3 compatibility behavior.

features.title: Content coding policy
features.description: Feature target Content coding policy imported from claims_registry.json.

features.title: Contract app dispatch
features.description: Dispatch contract-native applications through the native contract dispatcher.

features.title: Contract conformance tests
features.description: Test contract conformance across native and ASGI/3 compatibility paths.

features.title: Contract docs migration
features.description: Document the contract-native migration path and ASGI/3 compatibility policy.

features.title: Contract error semantics
features.description: Reject invalid contract scopes, events, bindings, and compatibility mappings with deterministic errors.

features.title: Contract examples
features.description: Provide examples for native contract apps and ASGI/3 compatibility apps.

features.title: Contract HTTP event map
features.description: Map HTTP events between tigr-asgi-contract and ASGI/3 compatibility events.

features.title: Contract HTTP scope
features.description: Validate and dispatch contract HTTP scopes with ASGI/3 scope compatibility where applicable.

features.title: Contract lifespan event map
features.description: Map lifespan events between tigr-asgi-contract and ASGI/3 compatibility events.

features.title: Contract lifespan scope
features.description: Validate and dispatch contract lifespan scopes with ASGI/3 lifespan compatibility.

features.title: Contract-native public API
features.description: Provide a public API for registering and serving contract-native applications.

features.title: Contract-native runtime
features.description: Serve native tigr-asgi-contract applications without ASGI/3 translation in the application hot path.

features.title: Contract release evidence
features.description: Record release evidence for contract-native and ASGI/3 compatibility support claims.

features.title: Contract WebSocket event map
features.description: Map WebSocket events between tigr-asgi-contract and ASGI/3 compatibility events.

features.title: Contract WebSocket scope
features.description: Validate and dispatch contract WebSocket scopes with ASGI/3 scope compatibility where applicable.

features.title: Contract WebTransport events
features.description: Implement WebTransport session, stream, datagram, and completion event handling on the native contract path.

features.title: Contract WebTransport scope
features.description: Validate and dispatch contract WebTransport scopes on the native contract path.

features.title: Canonical current-state chain
features.description: The promoted repository state is governed by one explicit human and machine-readable current-state chain.

features.title: Datagram flow-control mapping
features.description: Map datagram send behavior and carrier guarantees into contract completion semantics.

features.title: Default baseline profile
features.description: Feature target Default baseline profile imported from claims_registry.json.

features.title: Deployment profiles
features.description: Blessed profile artifacts are tracked in the SSOT registry alongside their validating tests.

features.title: Early data admission policy
features.description: Feature target Early data admission policy imported from claims_registry.json.

features.title: Early Hints contract map
features.description: Map HTTP 103 Early Hints behavior into contract metadata and ASGI/3 compatibility behavior.

features.title: Emit completion ASGI/3 extension
features.description: Expose completion metadata to ASGI/3 applications through documented extensions.

features.title: Emit completion events
features.description: Represent emit completion semantics using tigr-asgi-contract completion levels.

features.title: Fail-state registry
features.description: Feature target Fail-state registry imported from claims_registry.json.

features.title: Family capability declaration

features.description: Model datagram behavior as a contract-native runtime family.

features.title: WebTransport stream events
features.description: Implement WebTransport stream receive/send handling on HTTP/3 request streams.

features.description: Tigrcorn does not support WSGI as a product interface; ASGI/3 is the only supported compatibility layer.

claims.title: TC-CERT-RELEASE-GATE-GRAPH

claims.title: TC-CONTRACT-EARLYDATA-ADMISSION
claims.description: QUIC early-data admission is now explicit as deny, allow, or require, with deny as the default and explicit session-ticket advertisement behavior.

source_row.text: QUIC early-data admission is now explicit as deny, allow, or require, with deny as the default and explicit session-ticket advertisement behavior.

claims.title: TC-CONTRACT-EARLYDATA-APP-VISIBILITY
claims.description: Retry and 0-RTT interaction now has an explicit app/runtime visibility contract: transport state remains package-owned, ASGI apps see ordinary request scopes, and require-mode downgrade is surfaced as a package-generated 425 response before app invocation.

source_row.text: Retry and 0-RTT interaction now has an explicit app/runtime visibility contract: transport state remains package-owned, ASGI apps see ordinary request scopes, and require-mode downgrade is surfaced as a package-generated 425 response before app invocation.

claims.title: TC-CONTRACT-EARLYDATA-REPLAY
claims.description: Replay handling is explicit through the package-owned 0-RTT ticket replay gate, allow-mode post-handshake downgrade, and require-mode 425 Too Early rejection.

source_row.text: Replay handling is explicit through the package-owned 0-RTT ticket replay gate, allow-mode post-handshake downgrade, and require-mode 425 Too Early rejection.

claims.title: TC-CONTRACT-EARLYDATA-TOPOLOGY
claims.description: The multi-instance and load-balancer contract now states the honest package boundary: anti-replay is package-owned only per instance unless operators add shared coordination, so deny remains the honest default edge posture.

source_row.text: The multi-instance and load-balancer contract now states the honest package boundary: anti-replay is package-owned only per instance unless operators add shared coordination, so deny remains the honest default edge posture.

claims.title: TC-CONTRACT-ORIGIN-FILE-SELECTION
claims.description: File selection and HTTP semantics are now frozen as a package-owned contract: directory-to-index behavior is explicit, slash redirects and listings are not synthesized, MIME derivation and precompressed sidecar selection are deterministic, and HEAD, validators, Range, If-Range, 206, 304, and 416 behavior are aligned with the package-owned entity layer.

source_row.text: File selection and HTTP semantics are now frozen as a package-owned contract: directory-to-index behavior is explicit, slash redirects and listings are not synthesized, MIME derivation and precompressed sidecar selection are deterministic, and HEAD, validators, Range, If-Range, 206, 304, and 416 behavior are aligned with the package-owned entity layer.

claims.title: TC-CONTRACT-ORIGIN-PATH-RESOLUTION
claims.description: Path resolution is now frozen as a package-owned contract: percent-decoding happens once before normalization, parent-reference segments and backslash-separated segments are denied, mount-root containment is enforced after symlink resolution, and hidden names remain mount-relative rather than special-cased.

source_row.text: Path resolution is now frozen as a package-owned contract: percent-decoding happens once before normalization, parent-reference segments and backslash-separated segments are denied, mount-root containment is enforced after symlink resolution, and hidden names remain mount-relative rather than special-cased.

claims.title: TC-CONTRACT-ORIGIN-PATHSEND
claims.description: The ASGI pathsend contract is now explicit: the extension requires an absolute existing regular file, snapshots transfer length at dispatch, preserves best-effort zero-copy where available, caps growth races to the dispatch snapshot, and documents best-effort termination behavior for shrink and disconnect races after the response has started.

source_row.text: The ASGI pathsend contract is now explicit: the extension requires an absolute existing regular file, snapshots transfer length at dispatch, preserves best-effort zero-copy where available, caps growth races to the dispatch snapshot, and documents best-effort termination behavior for shrink and disconnect races after the response has started.

claims.title: TC-CONTRACT-PROXY-NORMALIZATION
claims.description: Forwarded-header parsing, root_path composition, and scope stripping now use one shared normalization contract across HTTP/1.1, HTTP/2, HTTP/3, and WebSocket carriers.

source_row.text: Forwarded-header parsing, root_path composition, and scope stripping now use one shared normalization contract across HTTP/1.1, HTTP/2, HTTP/3, and WebSocket carriers.

claims.title: TC-CONTRACT-PROXY-PRECEDENCE

source_row.text: Forwarded and X-Forwarded-* precedence is explicit for client, scheme, host, and root_path resolution across the package-owned HTTP and WebSocket scope builders.

claims.title: TC-CONTRACT-PROXY-TRUST
claims.description: Trusted proxy admission is explicit and fail-closed; proxy identity headers are ignored unless proxy handling is enabled and the immediate peer is trusted.

source_row.text: Trusted proxy admission is explicit and fail-closed; proxy identity headers are ignored unless proxy handling is enabled and the immediate peer is trusted.

claims.title: TC-DIFF-TLS13-STDLIB-CONTROL

claims.title: TC-SPEC-STRUCTURED-FIELDS-RFC9651
claims.description: Structured field parsing and serialization use RFC 9651 as the active baseline, provide deterministic package-owned parsing and serialization helpers, and lint active references so no obsolete predecessor baseline is presented as current.

source_row.text: Structured field parsing and serialization use RFC 9651 as the active baseline, provide deterministic package-owned parsing and serialization helpers, and lint active references so no obsolete predecessor baseline is presented as current.

risks.title: Interop and performance bundles could drift out of the release gate if they are treated as informal side artifacts.
risks.description: The mutable tree now carries explicit retained-bundle manifests that point at canonical release-root evidence and performance inputs.

risks.title: Structured-fields references can drift back to obsolete predecessor wording.
risks.description: Phase 8 replaces active predecessor-baseline language with RFC 9651 and lints for stale active references.

risks.title: New forward tests could continue to enter as unittest instead of pytest.
risks.description: Pytest is the only forward runner in CI, while legacy unittest files are frozen behind an approved inventory.

risks.title: Risk, claim, test, and evidence linkage can drift without machine-readable ownership.
risks.description: Phase 8 closes this by generating risk and traceability graphs and making release gates validate them.

boundaries.title: Authoritative Tigrcorn frozen boundary and governance graph

adrs.title: Canonical registry is a single JSON document
Features are the targetable units
Issues are plannable work items
Entity-centric registry with a derived graph export
Normalized prefixed IDs
Claim status and claim tier are orthogonal
Feature implementation state is separate from lifecycle state
Immutable boundary and release snapshots
Fail-closed guards
Generated projections are non-canonical
Explicit schema versioning
Portable SSOT core with repo-specific evidence adapters
Enforce max path and filename lengths under `.ssot`
Profiles are reusable feature bundles with derived pass status
preserve the ASGI boundary
separate transport and protocol
scheduler and backpressure are separate concerns
custom scope types are allowed behind the ASGI surface
doc governance tree
mutable and immutable marks
: Governance Authority
: Release Gate Graph
researched issue traceability through claim rows
adopt RFC 8785 JCS for canonical JSON
adopt WebTransport over HTTP/3 and QUIC as an in-bound contract target
adopt tigr-asgi-contract as the native runtime contract
preserve ASGI3 as a first-class compatibility layer
keep the ASGI3 adapter out of the native hot path
expose Tigrcorn features to ASGI3 apps through extensions
map ASGI scopes through contract scopes
map ASGI events through contract events
adopt explicit emit completion semantics
propagate contract unit identity through the runtime
expose transport metadata through the contract model
declare family capabilities with the contract model
enforce contract binding and subevent legality
implement WebTransport as a contract-native runtime surface
model streams and datagrams as first-class runtime families
classify SSE and server streaming through contract bindings
classify REST and JSON-RPC without owning application runtimes
select native or compatibility dispatch before serving traffic
track ASGI2, WSGI, and RSGI-like compatibility as bounded adapters
test contract conformance across native and compatibility paths
document the contract-native migration and compatibility policy
